page: Hetzner Cloud cax21 is an ARM-based virtual server belonging to the cax family, running on the QEMU hypervisor. It features 4 vCPUs powered by an Ampere Altra Neoverse-N1 processor clocked at 2.0 GHz, utilizing a shared CPU allocation model. The hardware configuration includes 8.0 GB of RAM, providing 1.95 GB of memory per core, and is bundled with 80 GB of local SSD storage. The processor supports hardware-accelerated AES cryptography but lacks advanced vector or matrix extensions such as AVX or AMX.

Benchmark results indicate average performance in standard computational tasks, with stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests scoring 1805.19 and 7235.85 respectively. Memory performance is mixed; the server achieves an average read-only large block memory bandwidth of 38.95 GB/sec and average database operations in PassMark at 2796.83, but exhibits weaker performance in write bandwidth at 25.37 GB/sec and overall PassMark memory score. Cryptographic and image processing capabilities are relatively low, as shown by a poor PassMark CPU encryption test score of 646.59 and a Geekbench image processing score of 1882.0.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the cax21 offers a budget-friendly entry point into ARM64 architecture, leveraging shared resources to maintain low operational costs. Due to its shared CPU allocation and moderate memory-to-vCPU ratio, this server is best suited for lightweight workloads such as basic web serving, development environments, simple database operations, and single-threaded utility tasks rather than intensive parallel computing or memory-heavy applications.
description: Hetzner Cloud cax21 is an ARM64 virtual server featuring 4 vCPUs on an Ampere Altra Neoverse-N1 processor clocked at 2.0 GHz, paired with 8.0 GB of RAM and 80 GB of local SSD storage. Operating under a shared CPU allocation model via the QEMU hypervisor, the server delivers average performance in standard CPU benchmarks, such as stress-ng, but exhibits weaker results in memory write bandwidth and cryptographic operations. With its 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the cax21 represents a budget-conscious option for ARM-based development. It is best suited for lightweight workloads, including basic web serving, text processing, and minor database operations, where high-density resource allocation is prioritized over dedicated compute performance.
og_description: Hetzner Cloud cax21 is an ARM64 virtual server featuring 4 Ampere Altra vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 80 GB of local SSD storage, offering a budget-friendly option for lightweight workloads.

tagline: An entry-level ARM64 virtual server with shared Ampere Altra processors and local SSD storage for budget-conscious workloads.

8.0 GB of RAM and 80 GB of local SSD storage
ARM64 architecture with hardware-accelerated AES cryptography
Average performance in standard CPU and database benchmarks
